title: Die älteste Fassung des me. Gedichtes 'Earth upon Earth'
summary: The repository XML copies University of Michigan CME item CME00138 and encodes only printed pages 50–61 of Max Förster's 1919 article, not the complete article on pages 39–61. Those pages form an edited selection rather than one medieval work: an eclectic five-stanza B-version reconstruction with an introductory couplet, two groups of added stanzas, a separate seven-stanza Oxford Corpus Christi text transmitted through A. S. Napier's working transcript, an independent nine-stanza poem from St John's E.24 collated through Murray against a Public Record Office roll, and a one-stanza sample of Murray's A version. Förster's inherited B-version readings are routed through Hilda Murray's 1911 edition rather than treated as direct physical consultation. Complete auditable facsimile objects are cached for Trinity R.3.21, Penn Oversize MS Codex 721, Brogyntyn II.1, Selden Supra 53, Balliol 354, and the current Trinity B.7.4 host of former B.15.39; selected-image caches with zero target overlap are explicitly partial.
review_notes: Förster's twenty-three B-version sigla denote textual witnesses used in the critical apparatus; they do not establish that he personally inspected twenty-three physical objects.

DIMEV v1.0 treats St John's E.24 ff. 44r–45r as one fourteen-quatrain B-version witness, while Förster separates the first five stanzas from the following nine-stanza poem. The conflict is preserved rather than resolved silently.

Förster's folio 233v for Oxford Corpus Christi MS 237 conflicts with the current DIMEV locator 240v; his Harley 2253 folio 57v conflicts with the current 59v locator. Both historical and current locators are retained.

Förster called the Stratford Guild Chapel mural destroyed. Current archaeological reporting describes the painting as extant, re-exposed in 1956, and among the chapel's best-preserved schemes, although upper and textual portions have suffered loss.
Access and rights statements apply only to the named entity or component. Public viewing never by itself licenses bulk download or redistribution.
Negative facsimile findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-13 and do not prove that no private, paid, or unindexed reproduction exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00138
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The XML has TEMPHEAD/IDG identifiers but no ordinary bibliographic header.
It encodes printed pp. 50–61; the article's pp. 39–49 are outside CME00138.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00138
entities.description: The public keyed electronic text from which the repository artifact derives.

entities.label: Max Förster, Die älteste Fassung des me. Gedichtes 'Earth upon Earth' (1919)

bibliographic.citation: Max Förster, ‘Die älteste Fassung des me. Gedichtes “Earth upon Earth”,’ Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 138 (Neue Serie 38; 73. Jahrgang) (1919): 39–61.
bibliographic.title: Die älteste Fassung des me. Gedichtes 'Earth upon Earth'
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen
bibliographic.publisher: Druck und Verlag von Georg Westermann

notes: CME00138 begins at article p. 50 and ends at p. 61.
Förster's five-stanza Urform is an editorial reconstruction, not a transcription of a single witness without intervention.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Archiv volume 138 (1919)
entities.reproduction_format: 296-page PDF with OCR and item metadata

entities.label: Hilda M. R. Murray, The Middle English Poem, Erthe Upon Erthe (1911)

bibliographic.citation: Hilda M. R. Murray, ed., The Middle English Poem, Erthe Upon Erthe: Printed from Twenty-four Manuscripts, Early English Text Society, Original Series 141 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, 1911).
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Poem, Erthe Upon Erthe
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 141
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: Förster cites Murray for most inherited texts and uses her version labels A and B.
Relations from this edition to witnesses express textual descent; they do not claim that Murray personally handled each present-day physical object.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 Earth upon Earth records
entities.description: Versioned catalog records 1170/IMEV 704 (B version), 1171/705 (C version), 6292/3939 (A version), 6293/3940 (nine-quatrain poem), and 6369/3985 (B version with introductory couplet), together with manuscript and bibliography data.

entities.label: A. S. Napier's working transcript of Oxford Corpus Christi College MS 237
entities.description: The modern working transcript from which Förster printed the complete seven-stanza Corpus Christi text. It is distinct from the medieval codex.

entities.label: Joseph Hunter's transcript in British Library Add MS 25478
entities.description: A nineteenth-century transcript at ff. 1r–3v, including the nine-stanza poem from the Exchequer roll at ff. 3r–3v. Helen Sandison identified the roll from this transcript.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS E.24
entities.description: Förster's newly used witness. The opening couplet and first five B-version stanzas are on f. 44r; a following nine-stanza poem continues through f. 45r. Förster treats the latter as an independent poem, whereas DIMEV 6369 describes one fourteen-quatrain B-version witness.

entities.label: William Yates–Bateman parchment roll
entities.description: A parchment roll formerly owned by William Yates and later the Bateman family at Lomberdale House. Förster reports a probable 1893 Sotheby sale but says Napier and Murray could not locate it.

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral MS 91
entities.description: Robert Thornton's paper manuscript; the five-stanza form is at f. 279r–v.

physical_description.extent: 322 leaves plus front and rear leaves; poem at f. 279r–v

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson C.307
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper manuscript with an Earth upon Earth witness at f. 2v and three otherwise unattested concluding stanzas.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9
entities.description: A fifteenth-century witness at ff. 67r–68v. Förster includes it in his B-version grouping, while DIMEV v1.0 catalogs its eighty-two-line text as the C version, record 1171.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.21
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper manuscript with the seven-stanza form at f. 33v.

entities.label: Complete Trinity R.3.21 IIIF facsimile
entities.reproduction_format: 686-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: University of Pennsylvania Oversize MS Codex 721
entities.description: The manuscript formerly known to Förster only as a Brighton antiquarian's parchment witness. Current OPenn description identifies the Earth out of earth poem and Latin sententiae added by a later hand on f. 91v.

entities.label: Complete OPenn facsimile of Oversize MS Codex 721
Oxford Corpus Christi College MS 237
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript with a complete seven-stanza Earth upon Earth text. Förster prints it from Napier's transcript and gives f. 233v; DIMEV v1.0 gives the current locator f. 240v.

physical_description.extent: Poem at current f. 240v; Förster's historical locator f. 233v

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1995
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript with a seven-stanza witness at f. 55r–v.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1671
entities.description: A parchment manuscript dated by the current catalog to 1375–1424; the Earth upon Earth addition is on f. 1*r.

entities.label: National Library of Wales Brogyntyn MS II.1
entities.description: A circa-1470 composite miscellany with the expanded witness at f. 79v.

entities.label: Complete NLW IIIF facsimile of Brogyntyn MS II.1
entities.reproduction_format: 425-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Selden Supra 53
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment manuscript with a witness at f. 159v; Förster notes omission of his stanza 5.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of Selden Supra 53
entities.reproduction_format: 338-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Stratford-upon-Avon Guild Chapel Earth upon Earth mural inscription
entities.description: A seven-stanza text painted on an angel's scroll on the west wall of the nave. Current archaeology reports that the mural survives and was re-exposed in 1956, correcting Förster's statement that it had been destroyed.

notes: The painting is associated with the late-fifteenth-century chapel scheme; surviving text and upper areas have losses.

entities.label: Balliol College MS 354
entities.description: An early-sixteenth-century paper commonplace book with the Earth upon Earth witness at current pages 434–435; Förster cites the former folio 207v.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of Balliol MS 354
entities.reproduction_format: 528-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached as a private-research ZIP

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 984
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript preserving only the end of stanza 6 and stanza 7 at f. 72r because the preceding leaf is missing.

entities.label: Magdalene College Pepys MS 2553
entities.description: A sixteenth-century paper miscellany with a Scots-language witness on p. 338.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ll.5.10
entities.description: A Scots-language copy made by John Reidpeth in or after 1622, apparently from the same exemplar as Pepys MS 2553; the witness begins at f. 43v.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment manuscript whose extended Earth upon Earth witness occupies manuscript pages 35–39 and supplies Förster's base for the second and third added-stanza groups.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 23
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century parchment manuscript with the extended witness at f. 111v.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.7.4 composite host
entities.description: The complete current composite physical manuscript represented by Trinity's 458-canvas IIIF object. Its part III is the former standalone B.15.39 and contains the Earth upon Earth witness.

physical_description.extent: Composite physical manuscript including current part III at ff. 165–172

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge B.7.4, part III
entities.description: The former standalone B.15.39, now part III of composite B.7.4 at ff. 165–172. The Earth upon Earth witness is at ff. 170r–v.

physical_description.extent: Former B.15.39 at ff. 165–172; poem at ff. 170r–v

entities.label: Complete Trinity B.7.4 IIIF facsimile
entities.reproduction_format: 458-canvas complete current host-volume IIIF Presentation object cached at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus A XXVI
entities.description: A composite paper manuscript with the Earth upon Earth witness at ff. 153r–v within the fifteenth-century ff. 143–207 unit.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson poet. 32
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript with an extended thirty-five-quatrain B-version at ff. 32v–35r. Digital Bodleian publishes only f. 30r and f. 57r, neither overlapping the work.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images for Rawlinson poet. 32
entities.description: The complete two-canvas provider selection, f. 30r and f. 57r. It has zero overlap with the poem at ff. 32v–35r.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4486
entities.description: A composite manuscript whose medieval parchment second part contains an Earth upon Earth witness at f. 146r.

physical_description.support: Parchment in the relevant second part

entities.label: The National Archives E 175/11/16
entities.description: A four-membrane Exchequer roll whose principal Ordinances material dates to the fourteenth century. The trilingual Earth upon Earth poem was added later at the top of membrane 4d; the record's 1311–1312 date belongs to the principal legal content, not securely to the poem.

notes: Harrington dates the later hand that filled blanks to the later fourteenth or early fifteenth century.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 913
entities.description: The principal witness of Murray's A version, seven six-line aaaabb stanzas at ff. 62r–63v. Förster prints only its first stanza through Murray.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF facsimile of Harley MS 913
entities.reproduction_format: 145-canvas IIIF Presentation 3 object cached through 1600-pixel-width requests; provider redirects and effective full responses are retained in the inventory
notes: Local technical preservation and fixity verification do not authorize redistribution; the unresolved provider reuse terms are recorded separately in rights:harley913-images.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2253
entities.description: A late-thirteenth- to early-fourteenth-century parchment miscellany with a four-line A-version parallel on current f. 59v. Förster cites f. 57v; DIMEV and the Project Gutenberg correction give f. 59v.

entities.label: Lincoln MS 91 catalog-selected image
entities.description: One repository-selected image of f. 80r. It has zero overlap with the Earth upon Earth witness at f. 279r–v.

rights.component: 1919 article text and typography as historical publication
rights.basis: Published in 1919, before the United States 1931 public-domain cutoff applicable in 2026; this does not determine rights in modern scans or repository encodings.
The ebook page states Public domain in the USA.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple distinct works, version units, witness texts, and apparatus
editorial_practices.representation: Five-stanza reconstructed B text, two added-stanza groups, complete Cp witness, independent C/Ro poem, and one A-version sample
editorial_practices.description: Förster deliberately selects and arranges several different textual units; CME00138 therefore cannot be modeled as one medieval poem.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twenty-three B-version sigla plus C/Ro readings
editorial_practices.representation: Dense note apparatus and eclectic critical readings
editorial_practices.description: The five-stanza text and added groups are editorial critical texts with readings from many witnesses; the independent poem is based on St John's C and collated against Ro through Murray.
editorial_practices.scope: Critical text and apparatus on printed pp. 50–60
notes: A relation to a witness indicates textual use, not necessarily Förster's direct physical inspection.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters printed in italic type and preserved as HI REND='italic' in XML
editorial_practices.description: Förster marks expansions typographically; the encoding retains extensive italic spans.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME00138 source XML at Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Establishes the repository artifact, exact electronic content, ID values, printed-page milestones 50–61, textual units, apparatus, and encoding practices.

evidence.citation: Programmatic and direct inspection of CME00138 XML structure and text boundaries.
evidence.locator: BODY; three DIV1 units; twelve PB milestones
evidence.summary: Counts 3 DIV1, 2 DIV2, 3 DIV3, 12 PB, 146 NOTE, 1,192 HI, 2 MILESTONE, 29 direct main-text LG/120 direct main-text L, plus one nested four-line Ro-only stanza in a NOTE for structural totals of 30 LG/124 L.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00138.
evidence.summary: Supports the public U-M electronic-text entity and CME identifier.

evidence.citation: Max Förster, ‘Die älteste Fassung des me. Gedichtes “Earth upon Earth”,’ Archiv 138 (1919): 39–61, complete volume scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 47–69; printed pp. 39–61; CME00138 at PDF pp. 58–69 / printed pp. 50–61
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the article bibliography, its selection boundaries, Förster's editorial method, all twenty-three B sigla and historical loci, the direct St John's and Napier sources, the independent C/Ro poem, and A-version sample.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and derivatives for archivfrdasstu138brauuoft.
evidence.summary: Supports item identity, derivative filenames, public download routes, and whole-volume cache provenance. The generic metadata date 1846 is not used as this volume's date; the scanned title page establishes 1919.

evidence.citation: Hilda M. R. Murray, ed., The Middle English Poem, Erthe Upon Erthe, EETS OS 141 (1911), Project Gutenberg electronic edition.
evidence.summary: Complete accessible source for Murray's witness texts, version labels, apparatus, historical manuscript designations, and the Harley 2253 frontispiece/corrected current folio.

evidence.citation: Project Gutenberg ebook 33768 item and current file inventory.

open_questions.question: Should the nine stanzas following the first five B-version stanzas in St John's E.24 be cataloged as a distinct work, as Förster argues, or as one fourteen-quatrain B-version witness, as DIMEV 6369 does?
next_steps: Inspect ff. 44r–45r directly, including layout, rubrics, hands, and any Latin/French parallels.
Compare Förster 1919, Murray 1911, DIMEV v1.0, Harrington 2016, and Nancy Pope's current edition/work analysis.
notes: This manifest preserves both analyses and does not force the second poem into the B-version work identity.

open_questions.question: What codicological or refoliation history explains Förster's Oxford Corpus Christi MS 237 folio 233v versus DIMEV's current f. 240v?
next_steps: Obtain the current College catalog description or images of the target and foliation sequence.
Locate Napier's working transcript and any annotations recording the old foliation.

open_questions.question: Does A. S. Napier's working transcript of Corpus Christi MS 237 survive, and where are its precise diplomatic/editorial features documented?
next_steps: Search Napier and Zupitza papers and correspondence.
Collate Förster's printed Cp text against current f. 240v images when obtainable.

open_questions.question: Where is the Yates–Bateman parchment roll now, and did it survive the reported 1893 sale?
next_steps: Identify the Sotheby Bateman sale catalog and trace lots and buyers.
Search manuscript sale/provenance databases using the incipit and Yates/Bateman/Lomberdale history.

open_questions.question: Beyond St John's E.24 and Napier's Cp transcript, which original manuscripts, working transcripts, or earlier editions did Förster directly consult for individual apparatus readings?
next_steps: Trace Förster's citations and Zupitza/Napier papers witness by witness.
Retain Murray as the documented general intermediary until a more exact route is established.

open_questions.question: Can Förster's tentative thirteenth-century and northern or North-Midland hypothesis for the reconstructed five-stanza Urform be supported independently of witness age and selected linguistic forms?
next_steps: Consult current work-specific linguistic, textual, and manuscript scholarship.
Keep composition date/place separate from St John's witness date and dialect unless the inference is independently supported.

open_questions.question: What historical foliation or printing error produced Förster's Harley 2253 f. 57v citation for the four-line A-version parallel now cataloged at f. 59v?
next_steps: Inspect current f. 59v and adjacent foliation when BL digital access returns.
Compare the 1911 print/frontispiece and earlier editions cited by DIMEV.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable physical-object facsimiles be obtained for the witnesses now represented only by catalog records, requests, unavailable viewers, or zero-overlap selections?
next_steps: Prioritize St John's E.24, Oxford CCC 237, TNA E 175/11/16, Lambeth 853, Rawlinson poet. 32, and the BL target leaves.
Request whole physical objects rather than target crops when feasible and record exact supply/reuse terms.
